Scope & Description:
Build and distribute pitchbooks for the 4 prioritized verticals to help sales reps meet target sales, covering each vertical's customer needs, pain points, hooking messages, and differentiation vs. competitors
• Manufacturing, Smart Order, healthcare, public institutions

Restore profitability on margin-dilutive existing contracts, especially on other financial-institution accounts (e.g., ATM) with negative CLTV ('NICE' Case)
• Negotiated to uplift monthly fee by 11M total
• Demarketed 4,000 VAN contracts (28% of total 14K)

Initiative Title: Raise monthly fees on existing customers and restructure new-customer contracts
Scope & Description:
Improve SKS profitability through systematic price increases for existing customers and price increase levers for new customers
• (Existing cust.) Opt-out based price increase requests
• (New cust.) Enhancement of base RMR setting, multiplier application
• (New cust.) Charing standard pricing for "currently free" add-ons
• (New cust.) T&C duration extension to, Programmed price increases

Initiative Title: Discover high-CLTV, high-value white space opportunities across captive and non-captive markets
Scope & Description:
Identify and validate high-value growth opportunities in emerging and underserved market segments:
• Emerging verticals with strong growth potential (e.g., data centers)
• Demographic and societal trends creating new security needs (e.g., elderly care facilities)
• Across non captive & captive demand opportunities across the SK Group ecosystem

Initiative Title: Set up a customer-centric product innovation and lifecycle management workflow
Scope & Description:
Set up a customer-centric product innovation workflow that:
• Detects emerging operational/security risks in a timely, systematic manner and integrates market insights into new product development
• Enables customers to proactively address emerging risks ahead of competitors, creating a sustainable competitive advantage

Bundle physical, cyber, and cyber-physical security with security-adjacent services into integrated enterprise packages
• Combine currently-siloed physical and cyber-physical security into unified enterprise packages
• Expand into untapped adjacencies (e.g., energy/utility monitoring, ESG/environmental compliance, occupancy/space analytics, EHS)

Scale standardized, low-cost, self-install (DIY) products for price-sensitive segments
• Standardize a low-cost product line without monitoring & dispatch for price-sensitive segments
• Replace rep-led design/installation with customer self-install
• Distribute through low-touch channels (online, retail, partners)

Advance high-value offerings from commoditized hardware to differentiated, tech-enabled solutions
• Shift high-value verticals from commoditized hardware to integrated solutions
• Embed advanced capabilities (AI analytics, predictive/preventive security, platform integration)

Initiative Title: Define customer tiers with standard management playbooks by value
Scope & Description:
Segment customer by CLTV and ARPU and deploy differentiated management scheme per tier to right-size effort to value
• [Tier A]: High-touch, hands-on management by regional strategic and branch sales reps, with regular review cadence
• [Tier B/C]: Centrally managed by dedicated CM team (see illustrative 2c), maximizing non-face-to-face and digital channels

Systemize proactive management for high-value accounts, to protect and grow the most valuable relationships
• Shift from reactive to proactive management
• Use early-warning signals (usage, payment, service, engagement) to flag at-risk and growth accounts before churn
• Drive account plans for upsell/cross-sell on high-value accounts

Initiative Title: Effective customer management through dedicated Customer Management team
Scope & Description:
Establish a dedicated CM team to efficiently manage the previously under-served B/C tiers and optimize in-house sales focusing on high-value activities
• Define CM team R&R, required FTEs and operating governance
• Implement C360 to facilitate customer insight and engagement

After the agency transition, assign top 20% performers among remaining sales reps with more than 50% of sales generated from prioritized verticals to Regional Strategy. Regional strategy will not only drive direct sales in prioritized verticals, but also act as PMs, supporting branches to expand high-value vertical sales

Set Gross Add targets by vertical for the 4 prioritized high-value verticals, sized from region X vertical penetration gaps.
Cascade vertical-level KPIs to regions and have HQ trackability
• Target set at vertical-average PTR for under-penetrated regions
• For Smart Order, target set at national market average self-order terminal adoption rate among restaurant clients by '28

Redesign sales incentives into a payout matrix that varies by two axes — monthly fee tier and product tier
• Payout rates scale up to 150% for higher-fee, high-value contracts
• Payout rates as low as 50% for low-fee, low-value contracts
• Incentive structure itself rewards contract value rather than volume

Initiative Title: Transition of in-house sales representatives to agency channel
Scope & Description: Convert low-performing sales reps across Regional strategy and Branch sales into high-productivity agencies, while supporting their successful onboarding and growth as agencies
Move beyond traditional door-to-door sales by scaling online channels under a clear CLTV-based pricing framework to expand geographic reach and customer coverage

Initiative Title: Widen sales-coverage areas by easing geofence limits
Scope & Description: Remove geofence restrictions to expand the reach of top-performers, while preventing free-riding and inflated performance recognition by assigning sales credit and SIP exclusively to contract owners

Initiative Title: Apply AI to detect and reduce false alarms
Scope & Description:
Minimize false alarm rate by adopting AI in detection to:
• Reduce false alarms and unnecessary dispatches while maintaining security effectiveness
• Reinvest operational savings into service quality improvements and broader operational excellence initiatives

Initiative Title: Adopt automation and AI tools to streamline back-office
Scope & Description:
Apply AI agents and RPA (Robotic Process Automation) to repetitive reviews and administrative tasks to:
• Capture a technology-enabled efficiency layer beyond Phase I organizational redesign

Initiative Title: Build SCM IT systems to optimize supply chain and logistics
Scope & Description: Enhance and develop related IT systems, including ERP, Blue Code, and Blue Supplier, to enable key functionalities required for integrated logistics operations (Initiative 6d), such as real-time inventory optimization and shipment tracking

Initiative Title: Improve ERP system to support the new GTM strategy
Scope & Description:
Transform ERP into the enterprise backbone that:
• Enables execution of the new GTM through integrated target-setting, performance management, and incentive mechanisms
• Consolidates data across core business systems into a single source of truth for decision-making and operations mgmt.

Deploy AI-powered automated monitoring capabilities that:
• Detect and prioritize security incidents in real time, enabling faster and more consistent responses
• Reduce monitoring-center workload and operating costs, freeing resources for service and operational improvements

Initiative Title: Revamp the security platform (IoT platform, Argos)
Scope & Description:
Build a unified security IT platform that:
• Seamlessly integrates signals and information across connected devices and systems, enabling faster, more accurate responses
• Optimize fleet-wide operations through intelligent resource orchestration, routing, and deployment

Develop and launch next-generation security solutions that:
• Proactively catches emerging customer needs and evolving security risks
• Deliver superior security outcomes through more efficient and effective technologies

Initiative Title: Construct CLTV single source of truth (SSOT) and decision-making rules
Scope & Description: Build a CLTV data foundation, since we have not measured CLTV before, and establish the rules and process for using it in decisions and keeping it continuously updated

Build a digital tool and dashboard (Vision360) that:
• Gives HQ and RHQ a region X vertical view of TAM, penetration, and whitespace to set and track branch targets
• Equips each branch with an actionable prospect list by vertical, so reps spend time selling rather than finding leads
Improve BP workforce efficiency through GPS-based workload optimization and predictive modeling, enabling data-driven realignment of dispatch territories and reducing FTEs

Initiative Title: Establish a holistic business intelligence system
Scope & Description: Consolidate fragmented data across function into a unified BI platform giving HQ, RHQ, and branch leaders a single, real-time view of business performance for data-driven decision making

Initiative Title: Revamp C360 to analyze customers, predict churn, and target retention/upselling
Scope & Description: Upgrade C360 into a proactive intelligence system that provides holistic customer insight based on interaction history, predicts churn risk and assists retention/upselling referencing customer information and sentiment

Initiative Title: Set up a data governance process
Scope & Description:
Establish data foundation enabling all data-dependent initiatives:
• Define data ownership by domain, setting quality standards and remediation protocols
• Setup governance body to enforce consistency across all data assets

Initiative Title: Orchestrate ERP and agency transition plan
Scope & Description:
Orchestrate a structured, sequenced workforce transition across two parallel tracks:
• Sales → agency conversion
• BP/TSE → natural attrition & hiring freeze
• Others → ERP

Initiative Title: Redefine R&R and evaluation scheme of field operations (BP/TSE)
Scope & Description:
Enhance operational productivity and workforce efficiency by the clear division of R&R between BP and TSE
• BPs were streamlined to focus on dispatch operations, while TSEs assumed responsibility for installation, AS service, and technical support activities

Increase operational efficiency and lower logistics outsourcing costs
• By centralizing material mgmt. and shifting from bulk deliveries to branches toward project-based delivery operations

Consolidate fragmented, duplicated, and non-core manual work into a leaner support organization
• 734 employees across 5 divisions, 5 HQs, 2 Deputy CFO organizations, 10 regional HQs, 21 groups, and 80 teams

Purpose — an objective baseline of structure and leadership capability before scaling change: not just how we are organized, but whether our people-managers and leaders can lead the change
• (1) Structure: span-of-control, layers & Org. Network Analysis across Physical · Cyber · Convergence
• (2) Leadership: define a success profile and assess people-managers & the leadership group against it

The measurable starting line for the high-performing-culture pillar
• Enterprise engagement & culture baseline survey: Vision, Leadership, Change mgmt, Org.Structure, IT/Tech, Talent & Development, Culture, engagement and values: SK Shieldus

Define what work is done, by whom, in what structure and governance
• Target operating model for the post-optimization organization — sourcing logic (in-house · BP · agency · AI), role architecture and decision rights, institutionalized

Revisit the performance management system
Goal cascading, role-based assessment, pay-for-performance and data-driven decisions on a psychological-safety foundation, sustaining a high-performing culture

Initiative Title: Win, build and upskill talent — 4B talent and leadership
Scope & Description:
Build the talent engine for sustained growth
• 4B: Build · Buy · Borrow · Bind — with critical-role succession, a leadership accelerator, AI/digital upskilling and EVP redesign
